title: Strict TSConfig, TypeScript 7 Defaults, and Project Graphs · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: Create a clean multi-package configuration that fails on unsafe boundaries, unused work, and incompatible module assumptions. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a strict TypeScript 7 project configuration and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Use strict, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, useUnknownInCatchVariables, and override checks from actual risk.
Account for TypeScript 7 strict, target, module, rootDir, types, and side-effect import defaults without legacy no-op flags.
Predict root files from files, include, exclude, imports, types, and library selection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Config Verification evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Config Verification assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A debug build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 6.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Config Verification has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Config Verification precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A debug build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 6.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Deleting random cache files is the first reliable configuration repair.
